agent_analysis.hypothesis_rationale: Larger organizations (higher total revenue) expend more money overall on fundraising activities (fees + event expenses) to maintain their donor pipelines.
agent_analysis.prior_art_notes: Weisbrod (1998) outlines scale effects on fundraising expenses.
agent_analysis.interpretation: A highly statistically significant but weak positive correlation was found. Nonprofits with higher total revenues spend more on fundraising expenses, but the proportion of incremental revenue spent on fundraising is extremely low.
